title: Formal Juhl–OR bridge
summary: Introduces abstract data asserting coefficient and normalized-expression agreement between a formal Juhl side and a formal OR side; no conversion from actual OR operators is implemented.

mathematicalContent.heading: Termwise bridge
mathematicalContent.body: A bridge term stores a Juhl coefficient/expression and an OR coefficient/expression together with proofs that both components agree.

mathematicalContent.heading: Right-hand-side bridge
mathematicalContent.body: At the finite-sum level the structure stores two formal right-hand sides and directly assumes their normalized equality.

mathematicalContent.heading: Current concrete construction
mathematicalContent.body: Only the reflexive bridge R↔R is provided. ORFormalizationData contains an order and a formal RHS but no actual OR operator or theorem connecting it to that RHS.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Stores matching Juhl-side and OR-side coefficients and formal expressions, together with their asserted equalities.
Stores two finite formal right-hand sides and a proof of normalized formal equality between their total expressions.
Constructs the identity bridge from a formal right-hand side to itself.
Placeholder data consisting only of an order and a formal right-hand side intended for a later actual-OR conversion.

references.locator: Formal Juhl-type formula and OR bridge
references.note: Primary source for the intended curved OR–Juhl identity.
Source for the Juhl side of the formal syntax.

limitations: The bridge structures contain the desired equalities as fields, and the only implemented bridge is reflexive. ORFormalizationData does not include OROperatorData, coefficients, evaluation, or a proof that any curved OR operator realizes the formal expression.
